coverage.notes: No C# code changes on this branch — changes are builder prompt (.llm), .pr files, and PLang test .goal files. Coverage analysis not applicable.

findings.issue: Builder generated inconsistent .pr for 'set %timedRetryAttempts% = %timedRetryAttempts% + 1'. TimedRetryGoal.pr uses variable.set with nested math.add object (type 'object'), while BareRetryGoal.pr uses math.add with return. Runtime stores the dict instead of evaluating the expression, so %timedRetryAttempts% becomes Dictionary<string,object> instead of int.
findings.impact: Timed retry test fails at assertion step 9: 'Actual: System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary`2[System.String,System.Object]'. The counter never increments — retries may actually be working but the test can't verify it because the variable is corrupted after the first set.
findings.suggestion: Rebuild TimedRetryGoal.goal — the LLM needs to produce math.add with return (like BareRetryGoal.pr) instead of nested object. Alternatively, split into two steps: 'set %temp% = %timedRetryAttempts% + 1' then 'set %timedRetryAttempts% = %temp%'. Known builder issue: same step text produces different .pr structures across builds.

findings.issue: ErrorGoalFirst test fails: %goalFirstAttempts% = 1, expected > 1. The .pr file has correct onError with order: 0 (GoalFirst) and retryCount: 2, but runtime only executes AlwaysFails once. Either the runtime doesn't retry after GoalFirst error handler, or the counter variable isn't shared across retry invocations.
findings.impact: GoalFirst retry ordering is completely untested — the test proves it's broken. This is either a runtime bug (retry not happening with GoalFirst order) or a variable scoping issue (counter resets per retry).
findings.suggestion: Investigate runtime retry logic for order: 0 (GoalFirst). Check if retries happen at all and if variables are shared across retry invocations of called goals.

findings.issue: BareRetryGoal.pr and TimedRetryGoal.pr have completely different .pr structures for identical step text ('set %var% = %var% + 1'). BareRetryGoal uses math.add with return, TimedRetryGoal uses variable.set with nested math.add object. This demonstrates builder non-determinism for the same pattern.
findings.impact: Even when bare retry passes (steps 0-4), the success is fragile — a rebuild could produce the broken TimedRetryGoal pattern. Test results are not reproducible across rebuilds.
findings.suggestion: Consider adding a builder post-processing step to normalize increment patterns, or use the two-step workaround consistently: separate math.add and variable.set steps.

findings.issue: ConditionCompound test fails with NullReferenceException. This test's .build files (app.pr, whennotequal.pr) were modified on this branch, suggesting a rebuild introduced a regression.
findings.impact: Pre-existing test broken by .pr rebuild on this branch. Not directly related to onError fix but indicates builder rebuild side effect.
findings.suggestion: Check if ConditionCompound was accidentally rebuilt during the builder rebuild. Revert its .build/ to the runtime2 version if it was unintentionally changed.

findings.issue: AlwaysFails.pr generated 3 actions for 'set %goalFirstAttempts% = %goalFirstAttempts% + 1': variable.get, math.add, variable.set. This is a third distinct pattern (vs BareRetryGoal's single math.add with return, vs TimedRetryGoal's variable.set with nested object). Builder non-determinism across all three increment steps.
findings.impact: Three different .pr structures for the same PLang step text. Only one (math.add with return) works correctly with the runtime. The other two either produce wrong types or may not increment correctly.
findings.suggestion: This is a builder prompt issue. The LLM needs stronger guidance on how to compile 'set %var% = %var% + 1' patterns consistently.

summary: C# tests pass (1511/1511). PLang tests: 65/68 pass, 3 fail. Two failures (ErrorRetryOnly, ErrorGoalFirst) are in new test suites added by this branch — they expose real issues: (1) builder non-determinism produces 3 different .pr structures for identical increment step text, only one works correctly; (2) GoalFirst retry order appears broken in runtime — retries don't execute. The ConditionCompound failure is a side effect of .pr rebuilds on this branch. The new tests are honest tests that caught real bugs, but the bugs they found need to be fixed before this branch can merge.
